steps.title: Identify the competition signal
steps.content: The key signal is the phrase “each strove for the mastery of the air.” The word 'strove' (struggle, compete) shows the breezes are competing for control.

steps.title: Match mastery to the choices
steps.content:
The underlined word mastery needs to mean the competitive control one breeze wins over the other, since each "strove" to achieve it. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Familiarity: having knowledge of something through experience — breezes striving for familiarity of the air makes no logical sense. ✘
  • Choice B — Domination: control or supremacy over something — breezes competing for dominance of the air fits the contest described. ✔
  • Choice C — Comprehension: understanding or grasping mentally — breezes cannot strive to understand the air; this applies to minds, not winds. ✘
  • Choice D — Skillfulness: high ability or expertise — breezes striving for skillfulness is incoherent; skill is a quality of performance, not something won in a physical contest. ✘
That matches choice B — B is correct.
